pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly lists Target: GRB 221009A in the generated specification.
setup_task.sh explicitly sets RA: 19.2175 in the spec, and verifier.py enforces abs(ra - 19.2175) < 0.1.
setup_task.sh explicitly sets Dec: 19.7733 in the spec, and verifier.py enforces abs(dec - 19.7733) < 0.5.
setup_task.sh maps Filter Simulator slot 4 to "R" and requests "Slot 4 (R-band)". verifier.py checks "R" in filt or "4" in filt.
setup_task.sh specifies "Exposure: 120 seconds". verifier.py enforces abs(exptime - 120.0) < 1.0.
setup_task.sh defines this path in the test example and export_result.sh checks this exact path for FITS output.
setup_task.sh instructs the creation of this script and export_result.sh specifically checks for its existence at this path.
setup_task.sh creates this exact file with the required specifications.
verifier.py docstring explicitly lists "Occupation: Telescope Control Software Engineering", contradicting the metadata.
Not explicitly assigned to "industry" in any script. The phrase is instead used as the "Occupation" in verifier.py.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates in a simulated environment using synthetic FITS imagery from the KStars/INDI Telescope and CCD simulators. The specific target parameters for GRB 221009A are explicitly detailed in setup_task.sh and matched in verifier.py: RA of 19.2175 hours, Dec of 19.7733 degrees, an exposure time of 120 seconds, and using the "R" filter on slot 4. The bash script must be created at /home/ga/too_capture.sh to produce outputs in /home/ga/Images/too_alerts/GRB221009A. A contradiction exists in the role definitions: while task.json claims the occupation is "Astronomers and Space Scientists", verifier.py explicitly documents it as "Telescope Control Software Engineering".
